properties.action_apr17: declared Hormuz 'completely open' for commercial vessels during ceasefire period
said US blockade 'will remain in full force' until peace deal; thanked Iran for Hormuz opening; said Israel 'PROHIBITED' from bombing Lebanon; expects deal 'in coming days'

properties.status_apr17: Iran declared 'completely open' with conditions; US blockade of Iran-bound shipping continues; only handful of ships transit; designated routes near Larak island

properties.detail: Araghchi: Hormuz completely open for commercial vessels during ceasefire period. Conditions: commercial only, no hostile country cargo, designated routes via Larak island area, coordinated with Iran Ports and Maritime Organisation
properties.response: Trump: 'thank you, but blockade stays in full force'

properties.detail: IRGC-affiliated Fars News citing SNSC source: Iran will close Hormuz again if US blockade not lifted. IRGC considers US blockade ceasefire violation.
properties.significance: Contradiction with Araghchi's opening — hardliner/pragmatist tension

properties.detail: CNN: Iranian sources say next round in Pakistan on Monday. Trump: expects deal 'in coming days'. Both delegations expected to arrive over weekend.

Lebanese army reports Israeli shelling of southern villages; machinegun/artillery fire at ambulance team in Kunin (Nabatieh); 1 killed in Kounine (motorcycle/vehicle struck); Israeli military warns residents to stay north of Litani River
After Netanyahu said Hezbollah fight 'not over', Trump publicly stated Israel is 'PROHIBITED' from bombing Lebanon during ceasefire. Rare public rebuke of Netanyahu.
properties.significance: US enforcement of Lebanon ceasefire against Israeli ally

properties.note: Biggest single-day drop since war began; WTI lowest since Mar 10; S&P 500 +1.2% weekly +4.5%, Nasdaq +1.5% weekly +6.8%, both at record highs

properties.detail: Lebanese civilians celebrate first day of ceasefire after 6+ weeks of war; 2,196 killed, 1M+ displaced in Lebanon since war. First quiet day since March 2.
Sharif arrives last leg of Saudi-Qatar-Turkey tour. Bilateral with Erdogan. FMs of Turkey, Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Egypt meet on sidelines to discuss war and Hormuz.

Iran agreed in principle to Pakistani proposal for third-party monitoring of nuclear programme by 4 countries + IAEA. Options for 440kg enriched uranium: send abroad or downblend to 3%. US has not formally responded.

Iran's 'opening' has 3 conditions: (1) commercial vessels only, (2) cargo/ships not linked to hostile countries, (3) designated routes via Iran's side (Larak island). Duration tied to ceasefire. Only handful of ships actually transit — shipowners wary.

properties.action_apr17: claimed Hezbollah fight 'not over' despite ceasefire; rebuked by Trump 'PROHIBITED from bombing'

properties.statement: 'no negotiating with clenched fists'; Turkey working to extend ceasefire and maintain talks

properties.action_apr17: called for continued talks and end to ceasefire violations
